title: glischrocolla formosa
parts: Glischrocolla formosa has many medicinal parts, such as its roots, leaves, and flowers, which have been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments. The roots of Glischrocolla formosa contain saponins, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which have been reported to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The leaves of the plant are rich in flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which have been shown to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. The flowers of Glischrocolla formosa contain anthocyanins, which have been found to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
side_effects: Glischrocolla formosa can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing severe gastrointestinal issues, including nausea and vomiting, due to its toxic alkaloids. Prolonged exposure to the plant's volatile oils may also lead to headaches, dizziness, and in rare cases, convulsions. Misuse of the plant can also result in an increased heart rate and blood pressure, posing a risk to individuals with pre-existing cardiovascular conditions. Furthermore, improper handling of the plant can cause skin irritation, including rashes and allergic reactions in sensitive individuals.
